title: Ranges Filter & Transform
code: auto even_squares = nums | std::views::filter([](int x){ return x % 2 == 0; }) | std::views::transform([](int x){ return x * x; });
description: C++20 range adaptors compose via `|` like Unix pipes. Both `filter` and `transform` are **lazy** — they produce a view object and only evaluate elements when iterated. No intermediate containers are created, making this both concise and memory-efficient.
